description: Build script dumps metadata into /home/user/builds/artifacts.db after each run — table called `build_artifacts` with columns `id`, `build_id`, `path`, `size_bytes`, `created_at`. Need to free up disk, so I want a list of the 20 largest artifacts. Just dump build_id and size_bytes to /home/user/builds/large_artifacts.txt, one per line, tab-separated, biggest first.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/builds/artifacts.db is a valid SQLite3 database.
- Table `build_artifacts` exists with columns: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, build_id TEXT, path TEXT, size_bytes INTEGER, created_at TEXT.
- Table contains 150 rows with varying size_bytes values (range roughly 1KB to 500MB).
- sqlite3 CLI is installed and available.
- /home/user/builds/ is writable by the agent.
- /home/user/builds/large_artifacts.txt does not exist initially.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/builds/large_artifacts.txt exists.
- File contains exactly 20 lines.
- Each line is tab-separated with two fields: build_id and size_bytes.
- Lines are ordered by size_bytes descending (largest first).
- The 20 entries correspond to the 20 largest size_bytes values in the table.

Invariants:
- /home/user/builds/artifacts.db is unchanged (no deletes, no schema changes).
- No other files created or modified in /home/user/builds/ besides large_artifacts.txt.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Output must match actual query results from the database — verified by running the same SELECT and comparing.
